app_info.subtitle: Search private vault files
app_info.description: iCloudPlugin Remote MCP helps users search a private indexed document vault, inspect generated notes and source metadata, review live system status, and trigger a bounded metadata refresh through ChatGPT.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only searches indexed private vault records and returns matching results without changing backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish, send, or modify public internet content or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only searches indexed records and hydrates bundled note and source metadata for the strongest matches without changing stored data.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not post or transmit changes to public internet state or external third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves live service, queue, classifier, and vault status from the private backend.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not modify public internet state or write to third-party services.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or trigger irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only evaluates current repo and runtime readiness signals and reports which end-to-end criteria are met or blocked.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or mutate public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves indexed metadata and item history for a previously recorded reversible change set.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or mutate public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves indexed metadata and member items for a duplicate-group proposal.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or mutate public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves indexed metadata and extracted content for one private file record.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or mutate external systems or public content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves file metadata and trims the returned content locally without changing backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not modify public internet state or third-party services.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves generated note content and note metadata for one indexed private file.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish, send, or change external systems or public content.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only returns canonical source paths, source links, and controlled download-handoff metadata for one file.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not publish or mutate public internet state or third-party systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Only retrieves file metadata, note content, and source reference data together for a single private file record.
justifications.open_world_justification: Does not modify external systems or public internet state.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete, overwrite, or perform irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Queues a bounded private metadata refresh job on the backing service, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only enqueues work on the private cloud-vault backend and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete data or perform irreversible actions; it only requests a new internal refresh run.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes internal backend refresh state by pausing background indexing while preserving resumable progress.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only updates the private cloud-vault backend control state and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete data or perform irreversible actions; it only pauses the current internal refresh loop.

justifications.read_only_justification: Changes internal backend refresh state by resuming paused indexing from the saved frontier.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only updates the private cloud-vault backend control state and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete data or perform irreversible actions; it only resumes internal refresh work.

justifications.read_only_justification: Creates a new structured Obsidian note in the private document_vault, so it changes internal vault state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only writes to the private synced vault backend and does not publish or transmit changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite existing source data; it creates a structured reversible note-side artifact.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs the local classifier only for one explicitly requested indexed file and writes a private document_vault note as a fallback, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only uses private cloud-vault backend services and does not publish or transmit changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete source data; it creates or updates a reversible note-side artifact only for the requested file id.

justifications.read_only_justification: Runs fallback local classification only for an explicit list of indexed file ids and writes private document_vault notes, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only uses private cloud-vault backend services and does not publish or transmit changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete source data; it creates or updates reversible note-side artifacts only for the requested file ids.

justifications.read_only_justification: Searches indexed private files and then runs fallback local classification only for the matched request-scoped files, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only uses private cloud-vault backend services and does not publish or transmit changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete source data; it creates or updates reversible note-side artifacts only for this explicit search-scoped request.

justifications.read_only_justification: Moves a live private vault file into _CHANGES_BACKUP and records a reversible change set, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only mutates the private synced vault backend and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not hard delete the file; it routes the change through reversible backup storage for later undo.

justifications.read_only_justification: Restores a previously backed-up private vault change set, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only mutates the private synced vault backend and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Restores previously backed-up files rather than hard deleting data or performing irreversible actions.

justifications.read_only_justification: Persists indexed manual-feedback event records from private document_vault notes, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only mutates the private synced vault backend and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite source files; it records reversible metadata about manual feedback signals.

justifications.read_only_justification: Analyzes live mirrored files and persists indexed duplicate-group proposals, so it changes internal backend state.
justifications.open_world_justification: Only mutates the private synced vault backend and does not publish or send changes to public internet systems.
justifications.destructive_justification: Does not delete or overwrite files; it creates candidate duplicate-group metadata for later review.

test_cases.description: Search private vault records for appeal-related files and notes.
test_cases.user_prompt: Search my private vault for appeal documents and show the strongest matches.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns bundled top matches with file metadata, generated note content, and source-reference metadata for relevant appeal items.

test_cases.description: Inspect the live system status before troubleshooting indexing.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me the current cloud-vault system status, including refresh progress and queue counts.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a consolidated status snapshot with service health, refresh progress, queue counts, and classifier readiness details.

test_cases.description: Inspect overall product readiness before claiming the rollout is complete.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me which cloud-vault product criteria are complete versus still blocked.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a consolidated readiness report with repo facts, live status summary, and explicit criteria marked met, blocked, or unknown.

test_cases.description: Retrieve a generated note for a known indexed file.
test_cases.user_prompt: Open the generated note for file 8213 so I can review the current classification context.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns note content and note metadata for the requested file id when a generated note is available.

test_cases.description: Retrieve source reference metadata for a known indexed file.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me the canonical source path and download handoff for file 23.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns canonical source metadata, source link information, and any controlled worker download URL for the requested file.

test_cases.description: Queue a bounded metadata refresh on the private backend.
test_cases.user_prompt: Trigger a metadata refresh on the private cloud-vault index service.
test_cases.expected_output: Acknowledges that a private refresh run was queued on the backing service.

test_cases.description: Pause a long-running metadata refresh without losing progress.
test_cases.user_prompt: Pause the cloud-vault metadata refresh for now but keep the saved progress so it can resume later.
test_cases.expected_output: Acknowledges that the private refresh loop is paused and that resumable progress was preserved.

test_cases.description: Resume a paused metadata refresh from the saved frontier.
test_cases.user_prompt: Resume the paused cloud-vault metadata refresh.
test_cases.expected_output: Acknowledges that paused refresh work was requeued or resumed from its saved progress state.

test_cases.description: Inspect one file with note and source context together.
test_cases.user_prompt: Get the full bundle for file 23 so I can review the file, note, and source reference together.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the indexed file payload, generated note payload, and source-reference payload together for the requested file id.

test_cases.description: Inspect one reversible change set and its indexed items.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me the indexed history for change set abc123.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the change-set metadata and indexed item rows for the requested reversible operation.

test_cases.description: Inspect one duplicate-group proposal and its members.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me the indexed duplicate-group proposal dup123.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the duplicate-group metadata and member rows for the requested proposal.

test_cases.description: Create a structured Obsidian note in the synced document vault.
test_cases.user_prompt: Create a structured document_vault note for this appeal file using the standard categorizer-compatible format.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns the created note path after writing a structured categorizer-compatible Obsidian note.

test_cases.description: Fallback to local classifier note creation when a sensitive file cannot be safely written through the normal ChatGPT-authored payload.
test_cases.user_prompt: The direct note-write path was blocked for this nursing progress note, so use the file id only fallback to create the document_vault note.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns fallback note creation metadata for the requested file id without requiring a sensitive title, path, or summary in the tool arguments.

test_cases.description: Safely delete a mirrored file by routing it into reversible backup storage.
test_cases.user_prompt: Delete Cases/Appeal.txt from google1, but make sure it goes through _CHANGES_BACKUP so I can undo it later.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a reversible change_set_id and backup metadata after moving the live file into _CHANGES_BACKUP.

test_cases.description: Restore a prior reversible file change set.
test_cases.user_prompt: Restore the change set abc123 from _CHANGES_BACKUP.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns restore metadata showing that the previously backed-up file change set was restored.

test_cases.description: Persist manual-feedback events from the live document vault.
test_cases.user_prompt: Re-read the Obsidian vault and persist the latest manual feedback events.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns a count of scanned and newly created indexed manual-feedback events.

test_cases.description: Analyze live mirrored files for duplicate candidates.
test_cases.user_prompt: Analyze google1, google2, and icloud for duplicates and persist candidate groups.
test_cases.expected_output: Returns created duplicate-group ids and candidate member sets without deleting or moving files.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for general weather questions.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: What is the weather in Anchorage tomorrow?
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because the request is unrelated to private vault search or status workflows.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for email-sending requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Send this note to my insurance adjuster.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it does not send email or publish messages to external systems.

negative_test_cases.description: Do not trigger for note-editing requests.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Rewrite the contents of my Obsidian note to make it shorter.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The app should not be invoked because it only creates structured notes and reversible file mutations, not arbitrary freeform note rewrites.
